pi_items.metadata_value: International Public Health / WHO Global Malaria Programme
pi_items.verified_value: International Public Health / WHO Global Malaria Programme
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh briefing header 'WHO GLOBAL MALARIA PROGRAMME'
setup_task.sh checks; downloaded directly from NOAA PSL.
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh output path variables.
setup_task.sh briefing mentions 'Sub-Saharan Africa'; Python analysis confirms strong spatial overlap in this region.
setup_task.sh briefing mentions 'Southern/Southeastern Asia'; Python analysis confirms strong spatial overlap.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires analyzing real-world NOAA NCEP Reanalysis datasets (air.mon.ltm.nc and prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc) to determine overlapping temperature and precipitation suitability for malaria. Independent python analysis confirms that 'Africa' and 'Asia' are the major geographic zones where July temperatures (20-30°C) and precipitation spatially overlap, directly matching the hints provided in the setup_task.sh briefing. All required input files, output paths, and the target month (July) exactly match the task description and setup/export scripts. A critical defect was discovered: 'verifier.py' is entirely missing from the task environment, rendering the task programmatically unscorable.
